title: 10 Herbal Creams to Help Elderly with Bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams have been used for centuries to help with bruising, and for good reason. The cream contains properties like thymol and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. These constituents help to reduce swelling and promote healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area. When you apply Arnica montana cream to a bruise, it helps to break down the blood clots that cause the discoloration, allowing your body to absorb them and return to normal.  To use Arnica montana cream effectively, gently massage a small amount into the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can do this as soon as you notice the bruise starting to form, or even up to a few days after the injury. Be sure to follow the instructions on the packaging and start with a small amount to test for any skin irritation.  By taking action and using Arnica montana cream as directed, you may notice a reduction in bruising and swelling within a few days. The discoloration may also fade faster than it would on its own, leaving you with less noticeable marks. The cream may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with bruising, allowing you to move more comfortably and get back to your normal activities sooner.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams have been traditionally used to help with bruises, and it's not hard to see why. The cream is rich in properties and constituents such as flavonoids, triterpene saponins, and carotenoids, which are powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents. These compounds work by reducing swelling, promoting blood flow to the affected area, and protecting the skin from further damage. When you apply Calendula officinalis cream to a bruise, it helps to calm the skin, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote the healing process. To use Calendula officinalis cream for bruises, apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin, making sure to cover the entire bruise. You can also use a bandage or a gauze to keep the cream in place. It's essential to be patient, as it may take a few days to see noticeable improvements. You might notice a reduction in swelling, a decrease in pain, and a faster healing time. The bruise may also change color, turning from purple to a lighter shade of pink or blue, which is a sign that the healing process is underway.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams, also known as witch hazel creams, have been used for centuries to help with bruising. This is because the plant contains a compound called tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Tannins help reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing by constricting blood vessels and preventing bacteria from growing. Other constituents, like gallic acid and flavonoids, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which further aid in the healing process. To use Hamamelis virginiana creams for bruises, apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can also use a cold compress or an ice pack to help reduce swelling before applying the cream. Gently massage the cream into the skin until it's fully absorbed. When you take these actions, you can expect the bruise to start fading and the pain to decrease within a few days. The swelling should also subside, and the bruise may turn a lighter shade of color. It's essential to continue applying the cream regularly to promote further healing and prevent scarring. Additionally, you can also elevate the affected area to reduce swelling and apply pressure with a bandage or a compression wrap.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams, also known as bilberry creams, contain antioxidants and anthocyanins, which are powerful compounds found in bilberries. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in bruised skin. The anthocyanins in bilberry creams have been shown to improve blood flow to the affected area, which can help to reduce the appearance of bruises. The creams also contain other beneficial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. To use a Vaccinium myrtillus cream for bruises, apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can also use a cold compress or an ice pack for the first 24 hours to help reduce swelling. Gently massage the cream into the skin, making sure to cover the entire bruised area. You may start to see improvements in the appearance of the bruise within a few days, and it may take up to a week or two for the bruise to fully fade. As the bruise heals, you may notice a reduction in the size and color of the bruise. The skin may also feel smoother and more even-toned. In some cases, the bruise may disappear completely, leaving no visible sign of the injury. However, the time it takes for the bruise to heal can vary depending on the severity of the injury and individual factors.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium myrtillus herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams have been used for centuries to help with bruises. The creams contain properties like allantoin, which is a natural compound that helps to break down scar tissue and promote the healing of skin. It also contains mucilage, a thick, protective gel that soothes and calms the skin. When a bruise occurs, blood vessels break and leak, causing the skin to turn blue or purple. The allantoin in Symphytum officinale creams helps to speed up the healing process by breaking down the blood clots and promoting new tissue growth. To use Symphytum officinale creams for bruises, apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin, making sure to cover the entire area of the bruise. You can also take a cold compress or an ice pack and wrap it in a cloth to apply to the area for 10-15 minutes, 2-3 times a day. This will help to reduce swelling and bring the blood to the surface of the skin, where it can be broken down and absorbed. After using Symphytum officinale creams for bruises, you can expect to see a reduction in the size and color of the bruise within a few days. The skin may still be slightly tender, but it should start to feel softer and less swollen. In some cases, the bruise may even disappear completely within a week or two. It's also possible that the bruise may leave a small scar, but the cream can help to reduce the appearance of the scar by promoting healthy skin growth.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams are topical treatments that can help with bruises. They work by improving blood flow to the affected area, reducing inflammation, and promoting the breakdown of blood clots. The creams contain flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which are compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling, pain, and discoloration associated with bruises. To use Ginkgo biloba creams for bruises, gently massage a small amount of cream onto the affected area 2-3 times a day. Continue for 7-10 days or until the bruise fades. You can also combine Ginkgo biloba creams with other self-care measures, such as applying ice packs to reduce swelling and elevating the affected area above the level of your heart.  As you use Ginkgo biloba creams, you may notice a reduction in pain and swelling within a few days. The bruise may also start to fade, and the affected area may return to its normal color. In some cases, the creams may also help to prevent the formation of new bruises by improving blood vessel function. However, the creams may not completely eliminate the bruise, and it may take some time for the affected area to return to its normal appearance.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams are known for their ability to help with bruises. This is due to the presence of properties like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The constituents, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to reduce swelling and pain. When you apply a Hypericum perforatum cream to a bruise, the cream works to: Reduce inflammation by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals Promote blood circulation to the affected area, which helps to remove excess blood and reduce discoloration Protect the skin from further damage by neutralizing free radicals To use a Hypericum perforatum cream for bruises, follow these steps: apply the cream directly to the affected area 2-3 times a day, gently massage it in until fully absorbed, and avoid covering the area with clothing or bandages. It's also essential to elevate the injured area above the level of your heart to reduce swelling. As you apply the cream, you may notice a reduction in swelling and pain within a few hours. The bruise may start to fade, and the skin may return to its normal color within a week or two. However, keep in mind that the healing process can vary depending on the severity of the bruise and individual factors.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams are a popular remedy for bruises, and for good reason. The cream is rich in properties like glycoproteins, vitamins A, C, and E, and amino acids that help to soothe and calm the skin. The main constituent, aloe vera gel, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that reduce swelling and promote healing. When applied topically to a bruise, the cream helps to break down blood clots, reduce discoloration, and promote collagen synthesis to repair damaged tissue. To use Aloe barbadensis cream for bruises, apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin to increase blood flow and promote absorption. You can also use cold compresses or ice packs to reduce swelling before applying the cream. It's essential to clean and dry the skin first to prevent any bacterial infections. Be patient, as it may take a few days to see noticeable improvements. When using Aloe barbadensis cream for bruises, you can expect to see a reduction in swelling and discoloration within 2-5 days. The cream may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with bruises. However, the rate of healing may vary depending on the severity of the bruise and individual skin types.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams are a popular natural remedy for bruises. The active compounds in Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain. These compounds also promote wound healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area and boosting the immune system.  To use Echinacea purpurea cream for bruises, gently apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can also massage the cream in a circular motion to increase blood flow. For best results, use the cream within the first 24-48 hours after the bruise appears. You can also combine the cream with cold compress or ice packs to reduce swelling and pain. As you use the Echinacea purpurea cream, you may notice a decrease in the size and redness of the bruise, as well as a reduction in pain and discomfort. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help speed up the healing process, allowing the bruise to fade faster. It's also essential to rest the affected area and avoid further injury to promote healing. With consistent use and proper care, the bruise should start to fade within a few days to a week.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for bruises

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have been used for centuries to help with bruises. These creams contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and swelling. Glycyrrhizin also has anti-oxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to improve blood flow, which can help to bring oxygen and nutrients to the affected area, promoting faster healing. If you have a bruise, you can try applying Glycyrrhiza glabra cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin until it's fully absorbed. You can also use a cold compress to help reduce swelling and ease pain. As the cream works to reduce inflammation and promote healing, you may notice the bruise start to fade and become less painful. The affected area may also become less sensitive to touch and pressure. As the cream continues to work, you can expect the bruise to become less noticeable and the skin to return to its normal color. You may also experience a reduction in pain and discomfort. However, it's essential to remember that bruises can take time to heal, and it's crucial to be patient and consistent with your treatment. It's also essential to follow the instructions carefully and use the cream as directed to avoid any potential side effects.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for bruises

intro_desc: As we age, our skin becomes thinner and more fragile, making us more prone to bruises. This is because the skin's natural cushioning layer, called subcutaneous fat, starts to break down, exposing the underlying blood vessels to injury. When we bump or fall, these delicate vessels can easily rupture, causing blood to leak into the surrounding tissue and resulting in unsightly bruises. For elderly individuals, these bruises can be more than just a cosmetic concern - they can be a sign of underlying health issues, such as poor circulation, fragile bones, or even malnutrition. As a result, bruises can affect their daily lives, limiting their mobility, causing pain, and even making everyday tasks a challenge. Elderly individuals may struggle to perform daily activities, such as walking, bathing, or even cooking, due to the discomfort and swelling associated with bruises. This can lead to feelings of isolation, dependence, and decreased quality of life.  To add to their woes, traditional treatments for bruises, such as ice packs and compression bandages, can be uncomfortable and may not provide quick relief. This is where herbal creams come in - these topical creams contain natural ingredients that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling, promote healing, and soothe the affected area. By applying these creams directly to the bruise, elderly individuals can experience faster relief from pain and discomfort, allowing them to resume their daily activities with greater ease.
